findings.description: The HTML does not use semantic heading tags to reflect the document structure; section titles and table titles are rendered as paragraphs instead of headings.
findings.evidence:
PDF shows clear structural titles such as 'MEMORANDUM', 'Table 1.', 'Table A1. Modeled Release Parameters for Point Sources', and 'Table A2. 1-hour SO2 and NO2 Modeled Emission Rates'. In HTML, these appear as

MEMORANDUM

and centered

elements rather than h1-h6 tags. No heading hierarchy is present.
